message:
feat: add binary body support to HTTP shim, sync runtime model on conversation reset, harden theme loading

HTTP shim binary body support (http_shim.rs, tests/node_http_shim.rs):
- Add __pi_http_is_binary_chunk() to detect Buffer, Uint8Array,
  ArrayBuffer, and other ArrayBufferView types
- Add __pi_http_to_uint8() to normalize any chunk type to Uint8Array
- Add __pi_http_chunks_to_base64() to merge binary chunks and encode
  as base64 via __pi_base64_encode_native for hostcall transport
- Add __pi_http_decode_body_bytes() to decode base64 body_bytes from
  hostcall responses back to Buffer/Uint8Array
- ClientRequest.write() now preserves binary chunks as Uint8Array
  instead of coercing to String, and the request payload uses
  body_bytes (base64) when any binary chunk is present
- Response handling decodes body_bytes back to binary when present
- Add 3 integration tests: post_buffer_write_sends_body_bytes,
  post_typed_array_write_sends_body_bytes,
  response_body_bytes_emits_binary_chunk

Conversation reset runtime sync (interactive/agent.rs):
- Call sync_runtime_selection_from_session_header() after
  ConversationReset to ensure the TUI model selector, agent provider,
  and thinking level stay in sync with the session header when
  resuming sessions
- Add test: conversation_reset_syncs_runtime_model_and_thinking

RPC cleanup (rpc.rs):
- Refactor .map().unwrap_or_else() to .map_or_else() in
  cycle_model_for_rpc for idiomatic Rust
- Remove unnecessary .clone() on runtime model entry in test

Theme loading hardening (theme.rs):
- Extract load_named_theme_from_dir() helper to eliminate duplicated
  project/global theme search logic
- When a project theme file matches by name but fails to parse, return
  an error immediately instead of silently falling through to the
  global theme directory — prevents a broken project override from
  being masked by a valid global theme with the same name
- Add theme_path_stem_matches() helper for filename-to-name comparison
- Add test: load_by_name_invalid_project_override_does_not_fall_back

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
